content:

author: jsign
content:
> But zevm thinks witness doesn't include history contract storage.
I would have to double check, but I think this would be expected considering that system contract writes previous block hash... and compared to any other storage slot write, it doesn't need to do a read since system contract doesn't have to charge for gas (thus know previous value of storage slot)?

author: jsign
content: Is zevm passing all the existing v0.4.1 tests?

author: omerfirmak
content: Is there any chance devnet is running a non-standard history storage contract?

author: omerfirmak
content: The slot BAL says the parent hash should be written to is different than what zevm is writing to.

author: omerfirmak
content: yes

author: jsign
content: Shoudn't from my perspective. Also if these artifacts are passing the specs stateless block run, if that was the case, it should fail the block validation too

author: jsign
content: So we might discover something interesting here

author: omerfirmak
content: I am bypassing the contract code and writing directly to the contract's storage. I will try to go thru the contract code and see if it fixes it.

author: jsign
content: Cool -- let me know after you dig a bit more, and if you conclude zevm is doing the right thing, I can try debugging that 57000-57009.tar.zst more carefully with the spec and see if there is anything weird.

author: omerfirmak
content: the error is pretty much the same for all blocks, so there is something obviously wrong 😄 Shouldn't be hard to track.

author: omerfirmak
content: Will let you know.

author: omerfirmak
content: 🤦‍♂️ found it, my eip2935 window constant was wrong.

author: jsign
content: Nice! This is awesome -- we got value (bug found in zevm) from this new devnet runs in guest programs pretty fast.

author: jsign
content: I think we still have to think why this wasn't surfaced by normal EEST tests

author: omerfirmak
content: Ah yeah, I think this devnet work is really valuable.

author: omerfirmak
content: probably because EEST tests never create chains longer than 8191 blocks.

author: jsign
content: but 2935 isn't about 8191 but 256

author: jsign
content: and I think this case was covered, unless i'm not understanding which was the bug on your side and how could have affected it

author: omerfirmak
content:
from eip 

Set the storage value at block.number-1 % HISTORY_SERVE_WINDOW to be calldata[0:32].

author: omerfirmak
content: HISTORY_SERVE_WINDOW is 8191

author: omerfirmak
content: my constant was 8192

author: jsign
content: Ah, I see. Yeah, okay that makes sense.

author: jsign
content: Def EEST can't do this long chain of 8k ish blocks without blowing up python impl.

author: jsign
content: Even the 257 case I remember was challenging to fill

author: jsign
content: Something feels odd here since this is then a blindspot even for normal stateful-ELs, no?

author: jsign
content: Some other existing non-8025 should be covering this

author: jsign
content: Maybe was simply decided it wasn't possible to cover this

author: omerfirmak
content: maybe. but I am surprised python spec can't even do 8k empty blocks.

author: omerfirmak
content: that is all it needs to do to cover this.

author: omerfirmak
content: btw, do you mind reminding me what EL spec release glamsterdam-devnet-5 corresponds to?

author: omerfirmak
content: I am getting 46 legitimate failures out of 100 blocks, I want to make sure I'm on the correct spec version before digging into them.

author: jsign
content: In the zkevm releases like https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/releases/tag/tests-zkevm%40v0.4.1 at the top I always put the "base branch" that I use, so in this case is (bal@v7.2.0)

author: omerfirmak
content: Got it, thanks. I was wondering it devnet-5 was still on an older spec.

author: jsign
content: So this is the filling of [these two tests](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/02c6c2510916e470f2c1e5191589212ca75d4948/tests/amsterdam/eip8025_optional_proofs/test_witness_headers.py#L353-L360) in parallel. So takes ~20s apparently to do the 256(+1) chain of blocks. So ~8k might be 10min filling only that test which prob won't be acceptable although doable.

author: jsign
content: I guess is also true that if you ever try any EL in a devnet/mainnet, your bug would be immediately observable (which is what actually happened)

author: omerfirmak
content: yeap, exactly. probably why people never bothered covering it in EEST

author: jsign
content: Was your bug also triggering a state root mismatch, no?

author: jsign
content: Since having the wrong modulo means writing to the wrong storage slot; so I'm still not sure I got why you identified this via the witness rather than state root mismatch.

author: jsign
content: Is this because the devnet block did some old read of a 2935 entry? And this isn't really coming from the pre-txs execution injection of prev header blockhash into 2935?

author: omerfirmak
content: hmm, probably because writing to a different tree leaf requires different nodes to be included in the witness.

author: omerfirmak
content: so state root couldn't even be calculated with this bug

author: omerfirmak
content: from state root calculation pov, writes are the same as reads, no?

author: omerfirmak
content: only during EVM execution you get to omit the read.

author: omerfirmak
content: oh, wait I see what happened.

author: omerfirmak
content: During state root calculation, I use BAL to figure out dirty storage locations. I read the latest value of a slot that BAL thinks is dirty from state and write that value to the trie. If that slot was never written to, that read fallsthru to witness, instead of being served by the in-memory dirty state.

author: omerfirmak
content: and witness doesn't include that value, because it doesn't need to.

author: jsign
content: Nice, makes sense!

author: omerfirmak
content:
figured out the other issue. https://github.com/omerfirmak/zevm/pull/34/changes/343d289b0d5765a37d66a53c7e126529d9f3c28e
I dont know who to ping but this case could be added to EEST.

devnet verification is green now https://github.com/omerfirmak/zevm/actions/runs/27781439381/job/82206963741?pr=34 will run it on an interval
